danger_signals.visual_form: Background spike traps, acid pools, abysses, flame effects
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-specific instant-kill zones; environmental danger that can execute defeated opponents

danger_signals.visual_form: Special character model designation (Shao Kahn, Kintaro visually distinct)
danger_signals.meaning: Increased threat level; boss characters cannot be finished with standard Fatalities, indicating greater power

danger_signals.visual_form: Dazed character state after life bar reaches zero
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent is vulnerable and critical—finishing move window is open

danger_signals.visual_form: Otherworldly stage design, hostile environment aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Geographic disadvantage for Earthrealm warriors; Shao Kahn's home field advantage communicated through visual alien architecture

safety_signals.visual_form: Non-lethal animations (dancing, gift-giving) performed after victory
safety_signals.meaning: Player choice to show mercy; truce or peace instead of execution
Humiliation without lethal consequences; comedic mercy rather than death

safety_signals.visual_form: Thunder god character present in narrative briefing
safety_signals.meaning: Divine protection for Earthrealm; supernatural ally status communicated through character inclusion

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Liu Kang, Sonya Blade, Johnny Cage, Kung Lao, Jax and other human warriors with Earth-origin aesthetics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament arena settings; Earthrealm warriors travel *to* Outworld (displaced from home territory)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shao Kahn (supreme ruler), Shang Tsung (sorcerer lieutenant)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mileena, Kitana, Kintaro—otherworldly visual design; demonic or inhuman character models
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outworld stages with alien/hostile aesthetics; home field advantage for Outworld representatives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shang Tsung character appears young/restored (visual restoration of youth after Shao Kahn's intervention)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shang Tsung demonstrates renewed confidence; Shao Kahn is actively recruiting and extending tournament invitation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple otherworldly stage environments; Outworld aesthetics dominate arenas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Earthrealm warriors are geographically displaced; fighting away from home communicates strategic disadvantage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent enters dazed state; visual UI indicates winning status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Winner gains access to finishing move window; opponent cannot defend or counter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Liu Kang defeats both Shao Kahn and Kintaro (narrative outcome)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Outworld invasion threat neutralized; Earthrealm protection maintained through Raiden's chosen warrior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bi-Han character replaced by Kuai Liang (younger brother); visual continuity of Sub-Zero character but with narrative succession
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scorpion's revenge arc established; character death has narrative consequences that carry forward

environmental_storytelling.example: Pit II stage features background spiked ceiling and figure in flames (later retconned as Blaze character); environmental hazards tell story of danger inherent to tournament location
Alien aesthetics in stage backgrounds communicate that Earthrealm warriors are in foreign, hostile territory; visual otherworldliness establishes power imbalance
Outworld characters (Mileena, Kitana, Kintaro) have visually inhuman or demonic features; design communicates faction origin and threat level without text

environmental_storytelling.technique: Finishing Move Variety as Narrative Choice
environmental_storytelling.example: Multiple Fatalities per character + Friendship/Babality options; visual finishing move selection communicates player agency over outcome tone (mercy vs. ruthlessness)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears youthful and energized; visual restoration indicates renewed power and confidence after Shao Kahn's intervention
Remains in Outworld, extends invitation to Earthrealm; strategic positioning emphasizes control and superior position
Gathers warriors and escorts them to Outworld; communication of duty to defend realm without direct combat participation in tournament
Character enters vulnerable dazed animation; visual immobility signals opening for finishing move execution
Cannot be finished with standard Fatalities (finishing moves blocked); behavioral restriction signals elevated threat tier and immunity to normal defeat mechanisms
Displaced from home territory; geographic disadvantage communicated through environmental context rather than stat changes

notes: Mortal Kombat II uses visual character design, stage environment aesthetics, and finishing move mechanics as primary manifestation layers for world-state communication. The game does not appear to use color-coding for factions in the Genesis port; instead, character model design (human vs. otherworldly/demonic aesthetics) and narrative context establish faction identity. Stage Fatalities are particularly significant as they visualize environmental hazard state. The dazed opponent state is a critical danger signal—visual cue for finishing move vulnerability. Boss character immunity to Fatalities is communicated through mechanical restriction rather than visual tell, but the distinction creates behavioral pattern recognition. The tournament being held in Outworld is the core world-state shift, manifested through stage design rather than HUD changes.
